raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "illustrating via contrastive example how a physician's verbal framing can trigger autosuggestive effects that either harm or improve patient outcomes",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "a physician's title alone confers suggestive authority capable of shaping patient belief",
    "declaring an illness incurable reliably installs a harmful autosuggestion",
    "framing an illness as serious yet potentially remediable reliably installs a beneficial autosuggestion",
    "the observed clinical results are produced by the autosuggestion itself rather than by other therapeutic factors",
    "positive framing can produce outcomes that exceed ordinary prognostic expectations"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a rhetorical contrast, but rests on an unexamined causal leap from verbal statement to durable physiological change without specifying mediating mechanisms",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the nocebo and placebo effects of physician communication must be strong, replicable, and clinically meaningful; patient outcomes must be sufficiently responsive to expectancy; and the statement must be used to encourage measured, evidence-based optimism rather than deception",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the dominant determinants of outcome must be biomedical rather than psychological; the claimed autosuggestive effects must be weak or absent; or the statement must be taken to justify withholding a poor prognosis when doing so would prevent informed consent or appropriate planning"
}

positive framing can produce outcomes that exceed ordinary prognostic expectations

parsed_json.conditions_for_usefulness: the nocebo and placebo effects of physician communication must be strong, replicable, and clinically meaningful; patient outcomes must be sufficiently responsive to expectancy; and the statement must be used to encourage measured, evidence-based optimism rather than deception
parsed_json.conditions_for_misleading: the dominant determinants of outcome must be biomedical rather than psychological; the claimed autosuggestive effects must be weak or absent; or the statement must be taken to justify withholding a poor prognosis when doing so would prevent informed consent or appropriate planning
